sparrow_project

Published

April 24, 2026

Research question:
How do insect abundance and ambient noise levels affect the number of fledglings per nest in house sparrows?

1 Prepare Session

For this exercise all the code will be available either directly or folded.

Code 
#importing libraries
  library(pastecs)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggpubr); theme_set(theme_bw())
  library(GGally)
  library(performance)
  library(MuMIn)
  library(ggeffects)
  library(tidyverse)

  library(knitr)  

#import data

library(here)

clean <- read_delim(
  here("data", "processed", "clean.csv"),
  delim = ";",
  escape_double = FALSE,
  trim_ws = TRUE
)

2 Explore Data

2.1 Numerically

Code 
sum_stats<-
   stat.desc(clean[, c("num_fledglings", "insect_abundance", "noise")], norm= T)

kable(sum_stats, digits=1)

2.2 Graphically

Code 
# Scatterplot matrix
  ggpairs(clean[, c("num_fledglings", "insect_abundance", "noise")])
Code 
# Draw a graph that captures the research question
  ggarrange(
    ggplot(clean, aes(insect_abundance, num_fledglings)) +
      geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black") +
      labs(x= expression("insect abundance (g/" * m^2 * ")"),
           y= "number of fledglings\n per nest "),
    ggplot(clean, aes(noise, num_fledglings)) +
      geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black") +
      labs(x= "Environmental noise (dB)",
           y= "Number of fledglings\n per nest"),
    nrow= 1, ncol= 2, common.legend= T, labels= c("a)", "b)"))

3 Fit models

3.1 Formulate and compare

As seen in Table 2 further analysis with glm.01 seems like the safest choice. This choice is based on the lowest AIC score and the highest Weight score.

Code 
# Fit binomial glms
  glm.00<- glm(num_fledglings ~ 1, family= "poisson", clean)
  glm.01<- glm(num_fledglings ~ insect_abundance + noise,
               family= "poisson", clean)
  glm.02<- glm(num_fledglings ~ insect_abundance * noise, 
               family= "poisson", clean)

# Compare models
  compare_performance(glm.00, glm.01, glm.02)
# Comparison of Model Performance Indices

Name   | Model | AIC (weights) | AICc (weights) | BIC (weights)
---------------------------------------------------------------
glm.00 |   glm | 152.4 (0.005) |  152.5 (0.008) | 154.1 (0.033)
glm.01 |   glm | 142.6 (0.726) |  143.2 (0.768) | 147.6 (0.834)
glm.02 |   glm | 144.5 (0.269) |  145.7 (0.224) | 151.3 (0.133)

Name   | Nagelkerke's R2 |  RMSE | Sigma | Score_log | Score_spherical
----------------------------------------------------------------------
glm.00 |       7.173e-16 | 1.323 |     1 |    -1.880 |           0.155
glm.01 |           0.943 | 0.326 |     1 |    -1.707 |           0.157
glm.02 |           0.944 | 0.324 |     1 |    -1.707 |           0.157
Code 
  kable(compare_performance(glm.00, glm.01, glm.02), digits= 3)

3.2 Validate preferred model

Code 
# First thing: check overdispersion
  check_overdispersion(glm.01)
# Overdispersion test

       dispersion ratio = 0.028
  Pearson's Chi-Squared = 1.020
                p-value =     1

no overdispersion detected

Code 
# Validate model
  check_model(glm.01)

The diagnostic plot in Figure 2 does not look too bad.

Code 
library(MASS)

glm.nb.01 <- glm.nb(num_fledglings ~ insect_abundance + noise,
                    data = clean)

compare_performance(glm.01, glm.nb.01)
# Comparison of Model Performance Indices

Name      |  Model | AIC (weights) | AICc (weights) | BIC (weights)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
glm.01    |    glm | 142.6 (0.731) |  143.2 (0.775) | 147.6 (0.863)
glm.nb.01 | negbin | 144.6 (0.269) |  145.7 (0.225) | 151.3 (0.137)

Name      | Nagelkerke's R2 |  RMSE | Sigma | Score_log | Score_spherical
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
glm.01    |           0.943 | 0.326 |     1 |    -1.707 |           0.157
glm.nb.01 |           0.943 | 0.326 |     1 |    -2.608 |           0.153
Code 
check_model(glm.nb.01)

4 Interpret preferred model

Code 
# Objects to construct table ------------------------------------------------

## Coefficients, SE, z and p -----
  coef.table<- round(summary(glm.01)$coefficients, 3)

## Approximate R2 -----
  r.2<- round(attributes(r.squaredLR(glm.01))$adj.r.squared, 3)  

## Compare performance to null-model -----
  null.chi<- anova(glm.00, glm.01)

# Objects to plot predictions -----------------------------------------------

## Interaction plot -----
  
  # Create predictions dataframe
  pred.int<- predict_response(glm.01,
                              terms =c("insect_abundance","noise"))
  
  
  # Create plot
  pred.plot<- ggplot(pred.int, aes(x, predicted)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin= conf.low, ymax= conf.high,
                    fill= group), alpha= .2) +
    geom_line(aes(col= group)) +
    labs(x= expression("Insect abundance (g/" * m^2 *")"),
         y= "Number of fledglings\n per nest", col= "noise", fill= "noise") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(pred.int$conf.high)))
Code 
# Create table
  kable(coef.table)
Code 
# Plot predictions
  pred.plot
